rationale: In the citing paper, Amir and Levav (2008) appears only as one item in a grouped citation supporting a general claim that heuristic processing can reduce preference stability. The paper's core theory, hypotheses, and experiments are built on money-versus-time processing and on other foundational sources, not on the cited paper's framework or findings. There is no direct reuse, extension, or explicit engagement with the cited paper beyond this background mention.
